privileged_info_summary: The task involves analyzing the real-world NCEP/NCAR Reanalysis dataset 'air.mon.ltm.nc' for surface air temperature. Analysis of the NetCDF file via Python verifies that the 'air' variable is natively stored in degrees Celsius ('degC') with an actual range of -62 to +39. However, the task prompt provides an incorrect instruction claiming the data is in Kelvin and must be converted to Celsius via °C = K - 273.15. The verifier checks for quantitative accuracy of expected Celsius temperatures (Tibet < 0°C, Andes > 0°C). If the agent blindly applies the formula, both values will be extremely negative (failing the Andes check). The agent must recognize the data is already in Celsius from Panoply or the file metadata and ignore the misleading conversion instruction.
